zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Atte Wode於1257年出生在英國英格蘭貝德福爾斯迪分頓時，她的父親William Wyckhurst Atte Wode34歲，母親Julia De Avenell27歲。她於1281年與John le Childe在英國英格蘭貝德福爾結婚。她去世於1309年在她的家鄉享年52歲。

nameMeanings.name: Atte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Tate (Old English Tāta, from tāt ‘happy’).

variants.name: Tates
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of Tate , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now very rare in Britain. In the US, it is found mainly among African Americans.

variants.name: Tade
meanings: Possibly an altered form of English   Tate  .

North German and East Frisian: from a Frisian short form of a personal name composed with the ancient Germanic element theud ‘people, race’ (see Tack , compare Thaden ). This surname is very rare in Germany and is apparently not found in East Frisia.
Japanese (pronounced as two syllables): written 蓼 ‘knotweed’. Not a common surname.

variants.name: Tait
meanings: Scottish and northern English: nickname for a cheerful person, from Middle English and Older Scots tait(e) ‘merry, eager, lively’ (Old Norse teitr), also used as a personal name. Compare Tate .

variants.name: Tasso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmine, Giancarlo, Gianpaolo, Lorenzo.  German Dieter.
Italian: topographic name for someone who lived near a prominent yew tree, Italian tasso (from Latin taxus, genitive taxi).
Italian: from the personal name Tas(s)o, derived from the obscure ancient Germanic element tāt, possibly akin to Old High German tāt ‘deed’. Compare English Tate .

nameMeanings.name: Wode
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Atte Wode was born in 1257, in Stevington, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Wyckhurst Atte Wode, was 34 and her mother, Julia De Avenell, was 27. She married John le Childe in 1281, in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom. She died in 1309, in her hometown, at the age of 52.

Elle a épousé John le Childe en 1281, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle est décédée en 1309, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 52 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Atte Wode(이)가 1257년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 스테빙턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Wyckhurst Atte Wode(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Julia De Avenell(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1281년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르에서 John le Childe(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1309년 그녀의 고향에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.
